slides.title: Spring Boot Architecture
slides.subtitle:
Mastering Enterprise Java
From Inversion of Control to Cloud Native Patterns

slides.name: Aravind Govindhasamy
slides.title: Software Developer
slides.experience: Specialist in Enterprise Java, Spring Boot, and Microservices
slides.oss_experience: Open Source Contributor & Technical Content Creator

box.content: The Core Pillars of the Spring Framework.
A design principle in which the custom-written portions of a computer program receive the flow of control from a generic framework.

list.text: Hollywood Principle: 'Don't call us, we'll call you.'
Decoupling: Object creation and lifecycle management is handed to the container.

box.content: A concrete implementation of IoC where an object's dependencies are 'injected' at runtime.
The central interface to a Spring IoC container, responsible for instantiating, configuring, and assembling beans.

list.text: Bean: An object that is managed by the Spring IoC container.
Configuration: Metadata describing how beans are created (XML, Java Config, Annotations).

box.content: Opinionated defaults for fast development.
Curated dependency descriptors that simplify your build configuration.
box.code:
<!-- Maven Dependency -->
<dependency>
    <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
    <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-web</artifactId>
</dependency>

box.content: Spring Boot automatically configures your application based on the dependencies found on the classpath.

list.text: @Conditional: Logic that decides whether to trigger configuration.

box.content: Package your application and its dependencies into a single, executable JAR file with an embedded server (Tomcat/Jetty).
box.code: # shell: java -jar target/myapp.jar

box.content: Abstraction over JPA and JDBC.
Spring Data JPA reduces boilerplate by allowing you to define repositories as interfaces.
Use annotations to map your Java classes to database tables.
Declarative transaction management using the @Transactional annotation.
Building robust RESTful APIs.
Use @RestController to build APIs that return JSON or XML by default.
Handle query parameters, body data, and custom response statuses.
Use @ControllerAdvice and @ExceptionHandler for consistent error responses across your entire API.

slides.title: HATEOAS (Hypermedia as the Engine of Application State)

box.content: Enhance your REST APIs by including links to related resources, guiding clients through the application's state.

list.text: Spring HATEOAS: Simplifies adding hypermedia to Spring MVC REST controllers.
Link Relations: Standardized ways to describe relationships between resources.

box.content: Comprehensive security for enterprise apps.
Spring Security is built on a series of Servlet Filters that intercept requests to enforce authentication and authorization.
Secure individual methods using annotations like @PreAuthorize.
box.code:
@PreAuthorize("hasRole('ADMIN')")
public void deleteUser(Long id) { ... }

box.content: Spring Security provides deep integration with Identity Providers (Okta, Keycloak, Auth0) using OAuth2 and OpenID Connect.

list.text: JWT: Stateless authentication using JSON Web Tokens.

box.content: Implement your own authentication logic by creating a custom AuthenticationProvider.
box.code: public class MyAuthProvider implements AuthenticationProvider { ... }

box.content: AOP, Events, and Scheduling.
Modularize cross-cutting concerns like logging, performance monitoring, or transaction management without cluttering business logic.
Decouple components using Spring's built-in event publishing and listening mechanism.
Easily run background tasks on a fixed schedule or using CRON expressions.
box.code:
@Scheduled(cron = "0 0 * * * *")
public void runHourlyTask() { ... }

box.content: Modern deployment and observability.
Production-ready features to help you monitor and manage your application.
Spring Boot natively supports building optimized Docker images using Buildpacks.
box.code: # shell: ./mvnw spring-boot:build-image

box.content: Tools for building distributed systems: Configuration Management, Service Discovery, and Circuit Breakers.
A community project to manage and monitor your Spring Boot applications via a beautiful UI.
Track requests across multiple microservices to understand performance and debug issues.
Manage application properties outside the codebase for different environments.
JUnit 5, Mockito, and Spring Test.
Test your business logic in isolation by mocking external dependencies.
Use @SpringBootTest to bootstrap the full application context for end-to-end verification.
box.code:
@SpringBootTest(webEnvironment = SpringBootTest.WebEnvironment.RANDOM_PORT)
class ApplicationTests { ... }

box.content: Focus on testing specific layers using specialized annotations like @WebMvcTest or @DataJpaTest.

list.text: Focus on testing specific layers using specialized annotations like @WebMvcTest or @DataJpaTest.
Testcontainers: Spin up real databases for integration tests.

box.content: GraalVM, Native Images, and AI.
Compile Spring applications to native executables for instant startup and low memory footprint.
box.code: # shell: ./mvnw native:compile

box.content: Deep integration with AI models (OpenAI, Azure, Hugging Face) directly within the Spring ecosystem.
box.code: ChatResponse response = chatClient.call(new Prompt("Explain Spring AI"));

box.content: Unified metrics and tracing for modern distributed systems.
Non-blocking basics with WebFlux.
The underlying library for Reactive Programming in Spring, providing the Flux and Mono types.

list.text: Flux: A stream of 0 to N elements.

box.content: A fully non-blocking web framework for building scalable applications on top of Netty.
Use R2DBC for reactive, non-blocking database access.
box.code: public interface TradeRepository extends ReactiveCrudRepository<Trade, Long> { ... }

box.content: The Future of Enterprise Java.
Spring Boot continues to be the industry gold standard for Java development.
Any questions? Thank you!
